---
title: "The Sacrifice Law Workbench"
---

::: {.hero-deck}
A comparative study of how political language makes nations, armies, and
ideologies sacred—and how killing and dying are made meaningful in their name.
:::

## The question

To what degree do recurring metaphor systems in leader-centered political
corpora support, complicate, or limit Koenigsberg's Law of Sacrifice, its
body-politic corollary, and the construction of enemies as bringers of death?

::: {.callout-note title="Current evidence status"}
All four pilot cases have draft support scores. The present comparison finds
the strongest support in the Hitler case and moderate support in the American
Revolution, Lincoln, and Napoleon cases. These are provisional analytical
results, not publication-ready findings: full-corpus review, human reliability
work, and publication-grade citations remain pending.
:::

The workbench tests the theory rather than assuming it. Similar metaphors do
not establish moral equivalence: historical endpoint, enemy construction,
institutional setting, and enacted violence remain decisive.

## Choose a reading path

::: {.grid .reader-paths}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .reader-card}
### Quick overview

Meet the four cases and see why they were selected.

[Explore the cases](case-selection.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .reader-card}
### Read the argument

Start with the provisional cross-case findings and their limits.

[Read the findings](cases/x-case/artifacts/qmd/x-case-overview.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .reader-card}
### Audit the evidence

Inspect status, methods, provenance, validation, and claim traceability.

[Open the research archive](research-archive.qmd)
:::
:::

## Four comparative cases

::: {.grid .case-grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .reader-card}
### [American Revolution](cases/am-rev/artifacts/qmd/case-overview.qmd)

Revolutionary founding, sacred liberty, collective obligation, and war.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .reader-card}
### [Napoleon](cases/napoleon/artifacts/qmd/case-overview.qmd)

Imperial glory, army–nation fusion, leader symbolism, and mobilization.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .reader-card}
### [Lincoln](cases/lincoln/artifacts/qmd/case-overview.qmd)

Union, covenant, battlefield sacrifice, providence, and reconciliation.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .reader-card}
### [Hitler](cases/hitler/artifacts/qmd/case-overview.qmd)

Racial purification, enemy construction, extermination, and genocidal
state violence.
:::
:::
